section: How it works · Earn → Tiers → Redeem
lede: Loyalty is a two-way ledger of trust: customers EARN points on what they buy and REDEEM them for discounts — and both sides resolve into the same order pipeline and the same books. Follow a point from the rule that mints it, through the tier it qualifies, to the till where it's redeemed — where the SERVER (never the client) reserves the points, turns them into a discount, and confirms the spend only when the sale actually lands. Every number here was captured from a real redemption.

steps.title: Loyalty is rules + tiers, not a toggle
steps.narration: The program is data, not hardcode. An EarningRule (order / category / action / tier-bonus) decides how many points a purchase mints; a LoyaltyTier decides status. The engine consults these live at order-paid time, so changing a rule changes tomorrow's earning without a deploy. The dashboard shows how many of each are active.

steps.title: Earning resolves at order-PAID, once
steps.narration: Points are credited when an order reaches chargeStatus = full — POS cash at the counter, or a web order paid later — NOT at placement. The order-loyalty-hook runs the active EarningRules against the order (here: 0.1 point per BDT) and credits the member. Per-rule idempotency keyed orderId:ruleId means a re-fire from either the born-paid or paid-later path can never double-credit. Points are earned once, when money is actually in hand.

steps.title: Tiers qualify on lifetime points
steps.narration: A LoyaltyTier carries a rank and a minimum lifetime-points threshold; the member's tier is whatever they qualify for. Tier-bonus earning rules can then multiply points for higher tiers, so status compounds. The current tier is denormalised onto the member document so the till can show it instantly without a lookup at the point of sale.

steps.title: Redemption is SERVER-owned (like promo)
steps.narration: At the till, scanning the member's card attaches them and reveals Redeem Points with their balance and a cap (here 15,177 pts ≈ ৳1,517). The cashier enters points; the client shows a DISPLAY-ONLY preview (points ÷ conversionRate = discount, capped at maxRedeemPercent). But the server owns the spend: pos.controller RESERVES the points — decrementing the member's balance — and computes the real discount. The client can't fabricate a redemption it didn't earn.

steps.title: Reserved points become totals.discount
steps.narration: The reservation's discountAmount is folded into the order's canonical totals.discount — the SAME field manual and promo discounts feed — so the kernel re-derives grandTotal = subtotal − discount. Here 500 points → ৳50 off ৳7,998 → ৳7,948. On order success the redemption is CONFIRMED (finalising the debited points); on failure it's RELEASED (the balance restored). It is idempotent, mirroring exactly the promo reserve/commit/rollback lifecycle — three discount sources, one pipeline.

steps.title: Books and balance move together (verified)
steps.narration: On the real sale captured here, two things resolved atomically: the order booked grandTotal ৳7,948 (subtotal ৳7,998 − discount ৳50) AND the member's balance dropped 15,177 → 14,677, exactly −500 points. On the ledger the redemption discount reduces recognised revenue (net Sales Revenue 4111) like any other discount — so a redeemed point costs the business real, tracked revenue, and can never be spent twice. Earn and redeem are two directions of one auditable points ledger, both settling through the order.
